# CSS Architecture Reference

Use this reference when making styling decisions, setting up theming, or choosing CSS patterns.
CSS architecture determines how maintainable your styles will be at scale.

---

## Choosing a CSS Approach

Follow the project's existing approach. If starting fresh, use this decision guide:

| Approach | Best For | Trade-offs |
|---|---|---|
| **Tailwind CSS** | Rapid development, consistent design, small teams | Verbose markup, learning curve, purging needed |
| **CSS Modules** | Component scoping, standard CSS, no runtime | No dynamic styles, verbose composition |
| **Vanilla CSS + Custom Properties** | Simple projects, progressive enhancement | Manual scoping, no built-in component isolation |
| **CSS-in-JS (styled-components, Emotion)** | Dynamic styling based on props/state | Runtime cost, SSR complexity, bundle size |
| **Panda CSS / StyleX** | Type-safe, zero-runtime, co-located | Newer ecosystem, build step required |

Rules:
- Never mix approaches in the same project without a clear boundary.
- Prefer zero-runtime solutions (Tailwind, CSS Modules, Panda) over runtime CSS-in-JS.
- In Next.js App Router, runtime CSS-in-JS requires `"use client"` — avoid for Server Components.

---

## CSS Custom Properties (Design Tokens)

Use custom properties as your single source of truth for design values.

### Token Structure

```css
:root {
  /* Colors — semantic, not visual */
  --color-text-primary: #1a1a1a;
  --color-text-secondary: #6b7280;
  --color-text-inverse: #ffffff;
  --color-bg-primary: #ffffff;
  --color-bg-secondary: #f9fafb;
  --color-bg-inverse: #1a1a1a;
  --color-border: #e5e7eb;
  --color-focus: #2563eb;
  --color-error: #dc2626;
  --color-success: #16a34a;
  --color-warning: #d97706;

  /* Spacing — consistent scale */
  --space-1: 0.25rem;
  --space-2: 0.5rem;
  --space-3: 0.75rem;
  --space-4: 1rem;
  --space-6: 1.5rem;
  --space-8: 2rem;
  --space-12: 3rem;
  --space-16: 4rem;

  /* Typography */
  --font-sans: "Inter", system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
  --font-mono: "JetBrains Mono", ui-monospace, monospace;
  --text-sm: 0.875rem;
  --text-base: 1rem;
  --text-lg: 1.125rem;
  --text-xl: 1.25rem;
  --text-2xl: 1.5rem;
  --text-3xl: 2rem;
  --leading-tight: 1.25;
  --leading-normal: 1.5;
  --leading-relaxed: 1.75;

  /* Radius */
  --radius-sm: 0.25rem;
  --radius-md: 0.5rem;
  --radius-lg: 0.75rem;
  --radius-full: 9999px;

  /* Shadows */
  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05);
  --shadow-md: 0 4px 6px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.07);
  --shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1);

  /* Transitions */
  --duration-fast: 150ms;
  --duration-normal: 250ms;
  --duration-slow: 400ms;
  --ease-default: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
  --ease-in: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1);
  --ease-out: cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1);
}
```

### Naming Convention

- **Semantic names** over visual: `--color-text-primary` not `--color-dark-gray`.
- **Component-scoped overrides** for local values: `--card-padding: var(--space-4)`.
- **Consistent prefix** per category: `--color-*`, `--space-*`, `--text-*`.

---

## Theming

### Light / Dark Mode

```css
:root {
  --color-text-primary: #1a1a1a;
  --color-bg-primary: #ffffff;
  --color-border: #e5e7eb;
}

[data-theme="dark"] {
  --color-text-primary: #f9fafb;
  --color-bg-primary: #111827;
  --color-border: #374151;
}

/* Respect system preference as default */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root:not([data-theme="light"]) {
    --color-text-primary: #f9fafb;
    --color-bg-primary: #111827;
    --color-border: #374151;
  }
}
```

```tsx
// React
function ThemeToggle() {
  const [theme, setTheme] = React.useState<"light" | "dark" | "system">("system");

  React.useEffect(() => {
    if (theme === "system") {
      document.documentElement.removeAttribute("data-theme");
    } else {
      document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme", theme);
    }
  }, [theme]);

  return (
    <select value={theme} onChange={(e) => setTheme(e.target.value as typeof theme)} aria-label="Color theme">
      <option value="system">System</option>
      <option value="light">Light</option>
      <option value="dark">Dark</option>
    </select>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, watch } from "vue";

const theme = ref<"light" | "dark" | "system">("system");

watch(theme, (value) => {
  if (value === "system") {
    document.documentElement.removeAttribute("data-theme");
  } else {
    document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme", value);
  }
});
</script>

<template>
  <select v-model="theme" aria-label="Color theme">
    <option value="system">System</option>
    <option value="light">Light</option>
    <option value="dark">Dark</option>
  </select>
</template>
```
```

Rules:
- Test both themes before shipping.
- Use custom properties for all theme-dependent values — never hardcode colors in components.
- Respect `prefers-color-scheme` as the default.
- Persist user preference to `localStorage`.

---

## CSS Layers

Use `@layer` to manage specificity without fighting it.

```css
@layer reset, base, components, utilities;

@layer reset {
  *, *::before, *::after {
    box-sizing: border-box;
    margin: 0;
    padding: 0;
  }
}

@layer base {
  body {
    font-family: var(--font-sans);
    color: var(--color-text-primary);
    background: var(--color-bg-primary);
    line-height: var(--leading-normal);
  }

  h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
    line-height: var(--leading-tight);
  }
}

@layer components {
  .card {
    background: var(--color-bg-primary);
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
    border-radius: var(--radius-md);
    padding: var(--space-4);
  }

  .btn {
    padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4);
    border-radius: var(--radius-md);
    font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
}

@layer utilities {
  .sr-only {
    position: absolute;
    width: 1px;
    height: 1px;
    padding: 0;
    margin: -1px;
    overflow: hidden;
    clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);
    white-space: nowrap;
    border: 0;
  }
}
```

Why layers:
- Later layers always win regardless of source order or specificity.
- No need for `!important` or increasing specificity with nesting.
- Third-party CSS can be contained in its own layer.

---

## Animation Patterns

### Transitions

```css
/* Base transition for interactive elements */
.interactive {
  transition: background-color var(--duration-fast) var(--ease-default),
              color var(--duration-fast) var(--ease-default),
              border-color var(--duration-fast) var(--ease-default),
              box-shadow var(--duration-fast) var(--ease-default);
}

/* Never transition all — it's unpredictable */
.bad {
  transition: all 0.3s ease; /* Don't do this */
}
```

### Enter / Exit Animations

```css
/* Dialog enter */
dialog[open] {
  animation: dialog-enter var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-out);
}

@keyframes dialog-enter {
  from {
    opacity: 0;
    transform: scale(0.95) translateY(8px);
  }
  to {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: scale(1) translateY(0);
  }
}

/* Dialog backdrop */
dialog::backdrop {
  animation: backdrop-enter var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-out);
}

@keyframes backdrop-enter {
  from { opacity: 0; }
  to { opacity: 1; }
}
```

### Respect Motion Preferences

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *,
  *::before,
  *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    scroll-behavior: auto !important;
  }
}
```

### Page Transitions (View Transitions API)

```css
/* Opt in to view transitions */
@view-transition {
  navigation: auto;
}

/* Customize transition for specific elements */
.page-title {
  view-transition-name: page-title;
}

::view-transition-old(page-title) {
  animation: slide-out 200ms ease-in;
}

::view-transition-new(page-title) {
  animation: slide-in 200ms ease-out;
}
```

---

## CSS Modules Patterns

```tsx
// Component.module.css
.wrapper {
  display: flex;
  gap: var(--space-4);
}

.title {
  font-size: var(--text-xl);
  font-weight: 600;
}

.title[data-variant="subtle"] {
  color: var(--color-text-secondary);
}
```

```tsx
// Component.tsx
import styles from "./Component.module.css";

function Component({ variant }: { variant?: "default" | "subtle" }) {
  return (
    <div className={styles.wrapper}>
      <h2 className={styles.title} data-variant={variant}>
        Title
      </h2>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Composing Styles

```css
/* base.module.css */
.reset {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  border: none;
}

/* Button.module.css */
.button {
  composes: reset from "./base.module.css";
  padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4);
  border-radius: var(--radius-md);
}
```

---

## Tailwind CSS Patterns

### Component Extraction

```tsx
// When a pattern repeats 3+ times, extract to a component — not a @apply class

// Good — component encapsulates the pattern
function Badge({ children, variant }: BadgeProps) {
  const variantClasses = {
    default: "bg-gray-100 text-gray-800",
    success: "bg-green-100 text-green-800",
    error: "bg-red-100 text-red-800",
  };

  return (
    <span className={`inline-flex items-center rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium ${variantClasses[variant]}`}>
      {children}
    </span>
  );
}

// Avoid — @apply defeats the purpose of utility classes
// @apply is acceptable only in base layer styles (global resets, prose defaults)
```

### Conditional Classes

```tsx
// Use clsx or cn utility for conditional classes
import { clsx } from "clsx";

function Button({ variant, size, disabled, className }: ButtonProps) {
  return (
    <button
      className={clsx(
        "inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md font-medium",
        "transition-colors focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2",
        {
          "bg-blue-600 text-white hover:bg-blue-700": variant === "primary",
          "bg-white text-gray-900 border border-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-50": variant === "secondary",
          "h-8 px-3 text-sm": size === "sm",
          "h-10 px-4 text-base": size === "md",
          "opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed": disabled,
        },
        className
      )}
      disabled={disabled}
    >
      {children}
    </button>
  );
}
```

---

## Vue Scoped Styles

Vue SFCs support `<style scoped>` for component-level CSS isolation.

```vue
<style scoped>
.card {
  background: var(--color-bg-primary);
  border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
  border-radius: var(--radius-md);
  padding: var(--space-4);
}

/* Deep selector — style child component internals (use sparingly) */
.card :deep(.title) {
  font-size: var(--text-xl);
}
</style>
```

Rules:
- Use `scoped` for component-specific styles.
- Use `:deep()` to target child component internals — but avoid when possible, it creates tight coupling.
- Use `:global()` inside scoped styles for one-off global overrides.
- Use `<style module>` if you prefer CSS Modules syntax with `$style`.

```vue
<!-- CSS Modules in Vue SFC -->
<template>
  <div :class="$style.wrapper">
    <h2 :class="$style.title">Title</h2>
  </div>
</template>

<style module>
.wrapper {
  display: flex;
  gap: var(--space-4);
}

.title {
  font-size: var(--text-xl);
}
</style>
```

---

## Common Utilities

```css
/* Screen reader only — must be available globally */
.sr-only {
  position: absolute;
  width: 1px;
  height: 1px;
  padding: 0;
  margin: -1px;
  overflow: hidden;
  clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);
  white-space: nowrap;
  border: 0;
}

/* Truncation */
.truncate {
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

/* Reset list styling */
.list-reset {
  list-style: none;
  padding: 0;
  margin: 0;
}

/* Focus ring — consistent across all interactive elements */
.focus-ring {
  outline: 2px solid var(--color-focus);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

---

## Anti-Patterns

### Do Not

- **Use `!important`** except in utility classes. If you need it, your specificity architecture is broken.
- **Nest more than 3 levels deep** in CSS. Deep nesting creates fragile selectors.
- **Use IDs for styling.** IDs have too-high specificity. Use classes.
- **Depend on DOM structure** for styling (e.g., `div > div > span`). These break on refactor.
- **Use `@apply` extensively in Tailwind.** Extract to a component instead. `@apply` is for base styles only.
- **Mix naming conventions.** Pick BEM, utility-first, or modules — not all three.
- **Hardcode colors, spacing, or font sizes.** Use design tokens / custom properties.
- **Use `transition: all`.** Transition specific properties — `all` is unpredictable and can be expensive.
- **Use pixel units for font sizes.** Users can't override `px` with browser settings. Use `rem`.
- **Write CSS that depends on JavaScript for basic layout.** CSS should handle layout; JS should handle interaction.
